commit.message:
fix: orchestrator dispatch uses wrong priority label names (#708)

* Initial plan

* fix: use correct priority labels (P1:high/P2:medium) in orchestrator dispatch and add stuck-issue re-dispatch

The orchestrator's backlog dispatch queried for labels 'P1' and 'P2', but
the repository uses 'P1:high' and 'P2:medium'. This meant the API call
returned zero issues every cycle, preventing any automatic dispatch.

Also adds stuck re-dispatch: if an issue is assigned to copilot[bot] for
>30 minutes with no open PR, the orchestrator removes and re-adds the
assignee to re-trigger the Copilot coding-agent webhook.

Closes #698

commit.message:
Fix improper indent on West Workspace and Project Config View (#433)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace path separators in tree item IDs to prevent incorrect indentation

VS Code's internal tree handle construction uses `/` as a hierarchy
separator. When TreeItem IDs contain `/` or `\` (from file paths or
as deliberate separators), VS Code infers phantom parent-child
relationships, causing incorrect indentation in tree views.

- WestWorkspaceView: replace both `/` and `\` in installPath IDs
- ProjectConfigView: replace both `/` and `\` in filename IDs
- ProjectTreeView: replace `/` separator with `.` in all IDs

.github/instructions/webgl.instructions.md — project-specific guide for

Covers: WebGL2-only policy, GLSL 300 ES required syntax, scene module

commit.message:
fix: healing monitor artifact path regression from #692 (#706)

* fix: heal monitor artifact path — download to workspace root not test-results/

Artifact 'playwright-results-chromium' contains test-results/results.json
as its internal path. Downloading to path: test-results/ caused double
nesting (test-results/test-results/results.json), so healing-monitor.js
reported 0/126 tests passing and exited 1.

Fix: download to workspace root (path: .) so test-results/results.json
lands at the expected location. Added continue-on-error: true so the
monitor job doesn't hard-fail if the artifact is temporarily unavailable.

Regression introduced in #692 (matrix parallelisation).
Closes #704

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

GHSA-6v7q-wjvx-w8wg: basic-ftp <=5.2.1 high severity
